title: 'Warning shot': Attack on UAE's n-plant was Iran's message to Gulf if peace talks fail
content: A drone attack on the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant, originating from Iraq, has heightened regional escalation fears. Analysts believe Iran-backed militias are a key front in Tehran's confrontation with the US and Israel. The strike, condemned as a terrorist act, forced backup systems at the plant to activate, highlighting the vulnerability of critical infrastructure amid rising tensions.

title: The game of numbers: When governor holds the key to people's verdict
content: In India's democracy, fractured election mandates often shift power to governors, who become arbiters of government formation. The recent Tamil Nadu election saw actor-turned-politician Vijay's party emerge as the single largest, but the governor's discretion in inviting him to form the government sparked debate over constitutional conventions and political arithmetic.

title: Google DeepMind CEO’s remarks on AI at I/O 'stunned' audience: What he said
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis told the I/O 2026 audience that humanity is "standing in the foothills of the singularity," drawing audible gasps. Speaking the next day, he said agentic AI systems are already making the shift tangible—pointing to mini video games he's built overnight with AI. Hassabis still pegs full AGI at a 50 percent chance by 2030, calling himself a cautious optimist.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.

title: 'If I was Agarkar, I would switch off my phone': Ashwin on Shubman Gill's T20I selection
content: Ravichandran Ashwin highlights the intense competition for India's T20I squad, praising Shubman Gill's IPL form but acknowledging emerging talents like Vaibhav Suryavanshi and Priyansh Arya. He notes the difficulty selectors face with a deep talent pool, including established players like Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma, making selection a significant challenge.

title: 'Big mistake': WHO warns against underestimating Ebola spread risk
content: WHO's Africa director warns against underestimating the Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda, stressing that even one case could spread widely due to the lack of a vaccine for this strain. He urged for greater international attention, highlighting the potential risk to all from a single infected individual. Efforts are underway to boost testing and community engagement amidst challenges.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: Parents’ emotional reaction to son’s Google job offer goes viral
content: A heartwarming home video of Harnav Singh sharing his Google job offer with his parents has captivated social media. Their genuine, emotional reactions, from his mother's proud embrace to his father's affectionate gesture, resonated deeply with viewers, highlighting the universal joy of parental pride and a child's success. This simple family moment became a viral sensation.

title: Surgeon removes wrong kidney: NCDRC awards Rs 2 crore compensation to family
content: A surgeon was found grossly negligent by the NCDRC for removing a patient's healthy kidney instead of the diseased one, leading to her death. The commission awarded ₹2 crore compensation to the family, calling the error a "medical disaster." The surgeon's defense was rejected, and institutional findings supported the negligence claim.

title: Viral moment! Virat Kohli erupts after Travis Head’s stumps are shattered
content: Rasikh Salam Dar's fiery yorker dismantled Travis Head's stumps, igniting a passionate celebration from the young pacer and a pumped-up Virat Kohli. Head's aggressive 26 off 16 balls ended abruptly, giving RCB a crucial breakthrough. Defending champions RCB, already playoff-bound in IPL 2026, faced Sunrisers Hyderabad in a key clash.

title: Dianna Russini’s marriage remarks fuel Mike Vrabel rumor buzz again
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and his alleged affair partner, Dianna Russini, have continued to dominate headlines as their affair rumors have refused to die down even after they denied everything. While Dianna Russini seems to have moved on in her life with her husband Kevin Goldschmidt, as they have turned heads on their recent outings, a confession of the former NFL insider has sparked chaos.

title: Wayne Gretzky reacts humorously to McDavid Oilers future trade speculation
content: Connor McDavid's future with the Edmonton Oilers is making big NHL headlines after another disappointing playoff exit. During a recent media appearance, Gretzky addressed the mounting speculation, joking that he doesn’t want Edmonton fans to damage his statue if he told McDavid to leave. The talk also turned to current NHL trade rumours, playoff frustrations and questions about Edmonton’s long-term championship hopes.

title: Sooryavanshi's Bihar to have IPL team? CM says govt working in 'mission mode'
content: Only four years ago, two new teams — Gujarat Titans and Lucknow Super Giants — were added to the league.

title: Students enraged over CBSE’s Class 12 evaluation, say OSM turned checking into a ‘lottery’
content: CBSE’s On-Screen Marking (OSM) system has come under scrutiny after Class 12 results triggered complaints of blurred scans, alleged evaluation errors and unexpected marks. While the Ministry of Education has defended the digital process as secure and efficient, students claim it has affected fairness and transparency, leading to distress and demands for clearer evaluation and rechecking mechanisms.

title: Carolina Hurricanes’ William Carrier faces awkward Canadiens rivalry at home
content: Carolina Hurricanes forward William Carrier says his wife and family are cheering for the Montreal Canadiens in the NHL playoffs. The surprise family feud has been one of the more fun stories coming out of the Eastern Conference Final. But Carrier remains 100 percent focused on helping Carolina keep its impressive postseason run going, despite the awkward playoff loyalties inside his house.

title: India tests Vayu Astra loitering munition, which can hit targets 100km inside enemy territory
content: Nibe Limited, a leading innovator in defense technology from India, has successfully tested its Vayu Astra Loitering Munition, which features a robust 10 kg warhead and a striking operational range of 100 km. This next-gen drone sets itself apart with its operator-controlled functionality, empowering users to identify and engage targets dynamically.

title: IPL 2026: Captain Rajat Patidar back for RCB; SRH opt to bat in Hyderabad
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins won the toss and chose to bat against Royal Challengers Bengaluru in their IPL 2026 playoff clash. Both teams have already secured their playoff berths. Cummins expressed a desire to test strategies and enjoy the game before the serious business of the finals begins. Rajat Patidar returns to captain RCB.

title: 'Amount is an insult': Pakistan hockey players hit out over PKR 1000 daily allowance
content: The PHF’s interim management announced that players and officials of the national team will receive a daily allowance of USD 110 during foreign tours.

title: 'Cockroach Awaam Party': Pakistan jumps on viral bandwagon
content: Inspired by India's viral Cockroach Janta Party, satirical groups like the Cockroach Awami Party and Cockroach Awami League have emerged in Pakistan. These social media-driven parties, adopting similar themes, aim to represent "lazy and unemployed" youth and Gen-Z, offering an alternative to established political factions.

title: Juhi Chawla's son Arjun Mehta graduates from Columbia university
content: Juhi Chawla celebrates her son Arjun Mehta's graduation from Columbia University, following daughter Jahnavi's similar academic success at the Ivy League institution.  Juhi shared joyful glimpses of Arjun's New York ceremony, highlighting his proud walk across the stage.  The family's academic achievements continue to shine.

title: ‘This is not MS Dhoni’s CSK’- Ashwin’s brutal reality check after disaster campaign
content: Ravichandran Ashwin believes Chennai Super Kings must invest in quality cricketers and a balanced squad for future IPL seasons. He stated the franchise can no longer rely on past strategies, emphasizing the need for experienced bowlers and spinners. Ashwin also questioned if the current CSK squad is suited for Chepauk's batting conditions, suggesting a more patient approach is required.

title: Man pays ‘zero tax’ on Rs 5 crore land sale despite filing ITR late; why ITAT gave tax exemption
content: Mumbai ITAT grants capital gains tax relief to a taxpayer who filed his Income Tax Return late. The tribunal ruled that reinvesting gains into a new property before a belated return fulfills Section 54 requirements, even without depositing funds in the Capital Gains Account Scheme.

title: SC to reconsider order denying bail to Umar Khalid, larger bench to hear matter
content: The Supreme Court on Friday chose not to comment on observations questioning its earlier bail denial to activists Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam. While hearing bail pleas for other riot accused, the bench indicated it might grant interim bail, but will consider Delhi Police's plea for a larger bench review on UAPA bail norms.

title: Iran’s rapid military rebuild: 7 weapons Tehran can mass-produce in months
content: Fresh US intelligence suggests Iran has resumed military production, including drones, much sooner than anticipated, raising concerns about its rapid arsenal regeneration. Tehran's strategy of decentralized manufacturing and reliance on commercially available components allows for swift replenishment of weapons like Shahed drones and Fateh missiles, even after recent strikes.

title: Twisha death case: Husband wants to surrender before court, says lawyer; why MP govt objected
content: Accused husband Samarth Singh has informed the Madhya Pradesh high court of his willingness to surrender in the Twisha Sharma dowry death case. His lawyer requested the trial court to hear his bail plea on the surrender day, a move opposed by the Solicitor General. The hearing also addressed a plea for a second post-mortem examination.

title: Why Allahabad HC had to suspend a mayor's powers to get a corporator sworn in
content: Lucknow Mayor Sushma Kharkwal's administrative and financial powers have been suspended by the Allahabad High Court for nearly five months of non-compliance. The court ordered the suspension until the Mayor administers the oath of office to a duly elected Samajwadi Party corporator, Lalit Kishore Tiwari, despite earlier directives.

title: Rajya Sabha polls on June 18: NDA may lose seat, Congress set to gain; TVK debut likely
content: Biennial elections for 26 Rajya Sabha seats will be held on June 18, with the BJP-led NDA expected to lose one seat. The Congress-led opposition could see its tally improve to five seats. The elections will cover 12 states, with bypolls also scheduled for Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Chiefs linked to Chargers WR after reunion buzz
content: The Kansas City Chiefs are being linked to former Los Angeles Chargers star Keenan Allen after fresh NFL rumors surfaced online. The speculation grew stronger after Chargers GM Joe Hortiz admitted the “door is not closed” on Allen’s return. While Los Angeles weighs a reunion, Kansas City could target the veteran receiver to give Patrick Mahomes another reliable weapon ahead of the 2026 NFL season.

title: 'Eternal will be memories': Guardiola to leave Man City after decade of dominance
content: Guardiola, who still had one year left on his contract, will take charge of his final match against Aston Villa in the Premier League on Sunday.

title: 'Marwa denge tujhe': Cockroach Janta Party founder receives death threats
content: Founder of the satirical Cockroach Janta Party, Abhijeet Dipke, has reported receiving death threats on WhatsApp, urging him to shut down the viral social media outfit or join the BJP. His parents have also voiced concerns about his safety and potential arrest due to the party's rapid online popularity.

title: Trump to send 5,000 additional troops to fortress Poland to further strengthen Nato's eastern flank
content: The US is bolstering its presence in Poland with an additional 5,000 troops, recognizing Warsaw's escalating importance in regional security. Poland, already hosting 10,000 American soldiers, is rapidly militarizing, investing heavily in advanced weaponry from the US and South Korea.

title: Biodiversity begins at home: How local action can create global impact
content: Biodiversity is crucial for human survival, yet it's declining rapidly due to human actions. India is strengthening its conservation efforts, with local communities playing a vital role. Initiatives like restoring coastal ecosystems and creating micro-forests demonstrate how grassroots actions, empowered by traditional knowledge and community institutions, significantly contribute to global biodiversity goals and sustainable livelihoods.

title: ‘Now that cremation is over...’: Ex-cricketer’s shocking disclosure about commentary
content: Former cricketer Laxman Sivaramakrishnan revealed he was compelled to commentate shortly after his brother's cremation, highlighting immense professional pressure. He also cited this and alleged colour discrimination as reasons for his recent retirement from the BCCI commentary panel, detailing past struggles with mental health and appearance-based bias.

title: Excluding family from your Will won't make it invalid, Supreme Court rules
content: The Supreme Court has underscored the legal rights of individuals to dictate their estate plans, stating that a Will is not automatically deemed suspicious simply because it omits heirs like a spouse or children.

title: ‘Bringing war back to Russia’: Ukraine strikes Yaroslavl oil refinery
content: Ukraine has escalated its offensive by targeting a Russian oil refinery situated in Yaroslavl, roughly 700 kilometers away from its own territory, as declared by President Zelenskyy. He positioned this response as a fair counteraction in the ongoing conflict, propelling the conflict back into Russian territory.

title: TANCET 2026 result released, scorecards to be released on this date
content: Anna University has announced the TANCET Result 2026. Candidates can check their scores on the official website. Over 30,000 students appeared for the TANCET and CEETA PG examinations. Scorecards will be available from May 27. Profile corrections are permitted before downloading the scorecard. Further updates on counselling will be provided.

title: Twisha Sharma death: Mother-in-law’s lawyer loses cool, beats vehicle bonnet; watch
content: Lawyer for Twisha Sharma's mother-in-law, Giribala Singh, Advocate Enosh George, had a heated exchange with journalists outside the residence. After his vehicle allegedly ran over his foot, George accused reporters of creating a dangerous situation and questioned the impact of their legal responses in the ongoing dowry death investigation.

title: Over 2.3 lakh students register for TNEA 2026
content: Counselling likely to begin in second week of July, starting with sessions for special category students, say officials

title: Are cartoons in NCERT textbooks proper? Supreme Court asks retired judge panel to review
content: The order came after Solicitor General Tushar Mehta raised the issue and said 'textbook is not a place where you use cartoons'

title: Plus One admissions likely from May 25
content: At present, 3,60,844 higher secondary seats, 33,000 vocational higher secondary seats, and over 30,000 seats in polytechnic and ITIs are available in the State

title: Election Commission announces Rajya Sabha polls for 24 seats on June 18
content: The last date for filing nominations is June 8 and counting will take place after polling on the same day

title: Modi afraid of satire: K. Prakash Babu
content: CPI leader slams PM for blocking satirical account, accuses Centre of undermining workers and democracy

title: Lack of Aadhaar casts shadow over education of tribal students in Edamalakkudy in Kerala
content: Without Aadhaar numbers, school authorities cannot register students’ name on government portal. Though they can be enrolled, the students will not receive any benefits

title: Delhi riots 2020 case: Umar Khalid gets three-day interim bail
content: The court permitted Mr. Khalid to be released from June 1 to June 3 to enable him to meet his mother who has to undergo a surgery

title: Jaishankar to host Quad Foreign Ministers meet on May 26: MEA
content: Indo-Pacific grouping expected to discuss U.S. President Trump’s China visit, the Iran war, and the Hormuz blockade; U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio to arrive on May 23 for four-day bilateral talks

title: Kerala’s maternal mortality ratio now stands at 24
content: Kerala continues to be the State with the lowest maternal mortality ratio in the country. At the national level, the ratio stands at 87

title: Who was protecting Bandi Sanjay’s son after being named as accused in POCSO case, asks KTR
content: The BRS leader further wanted the Union Minister and the Chief Minister to explain to the people of Telangana as to who had managed the media either for not giving coverage to the news at all or to dismiss it as a petty issue

title: Lokesh lauds progressive farmer on achieving success in natural farming
content: Urging farmers to adopt organic farming practices, the IT Minister says Zero Budget Natural Farming remains the core vision of the Chief Minister

title: Telangana’s NIMS doctors perform a record 2,000 kidney transplants, 95% free of cost
content: With the milestone, NIMS emerged as one of the country’s highest-volume renal transplant centres in government sector

title: Maharashtra's economy to surpass those of Singapore, UAE in 2 to 3 years: Fadnavis
content: Maharashtra was not competing with any other Indian State as it has already become the world's 30th largest economy, Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis said

title: Nurdle menace persists a year after MSC Elsa 3 disaster off Kerala coast
content: Despite clean-up efforts made in the immediate aftermath of the nurdle landings, large numbers of the plastic pellets are still visible on beaches in Thiruvananthapuram. Coastal residents and Environment department officials say beach clean-up by a firm tasked with onshore salvage has ceased

title: Lokesh takes a dig at Jagan and YSRCP during Kadapa visit
content: ‘Our brand is job creation, your brand is axe’, says the TDP national working president, and insists that Andhra Pradesh owes its development to ‘Brand CBN’

title: Kerala High Court asks why nursing is not declared ‘essential service’
content: A petition filed by the Kerala State United Nurses’ Association had challenged the appointment of unregistered persons as nurses in two hospitals during an ongoing strike by qualified nurses

title: Chandrababu Naidu tells officials to issue continuous heatwave alerts as mercury soars to 48°C in A.P.
content: Authorities instructed to intensify awareness campaigns on heatstroke and emergency preparedness measures, ensure availability of drinking water, buttermilk and ORS sachets at public places

title: BJP's Bengal win can reshape India's economic direction: Pradeep Gupta
content: Pradeep Gupta pointed out that regions with a strong port infrastructure and trade networks historically became centres of prosperity

title: Two held for assault on village officer, assistant in Thrissur
content: The officials question the accused on noticing illegal loading of soil into a tipper lorry, leading to an altercation. The first accused then allegedly drives a tipper lorry towards the officials in an attempt to run them over, but they escaped unharmed.

title: Kerala Cabinet: Latin Catholic Church remains dissatisfied with exclusion; Fisheries Minister meets Archbishop
content: IUML leader and Fisheries Minister Abdul Gafoor’s mission to iron out difficulties with Church leadership in a closed-door meet with Thiruvananthapuram Archbishop Thomas J. Netto appears to have yielded mixed results

title: TVK's Win In Tamil Nadu Sparks Major Alliance Realignment Across Parties
content: The first major setback for the DMK came soon after the results when its long-time ally, the Indian National Congress, snapped ties and aligned with TVK, along with its five MLAs.

title: BJP's Nationwide Blueprint To Mark 12 Years Of PM Modi-Led Government
content: Senior Union ministers, BJP leaders, chief ministers, MPs and party office bearers will fan out across the country over the coming weeks to present the government's report card.

title: Cockroach Janta Party Crosses Border, Pakistan Now 'Infested'
content: There are many versions of the Cockroach Janta Party in Pakistan, such as Cockroach Awami Party, Cockroach Awami League and Muttahida Cockroach Movement. The bio of the Cockroach Awami Party reads, "A political front of the youth, by the youth, for P

title: Opinion: Opinion | Degrees, SUVs, Gold: Why India's Most 'Qualified' Men Often Demand The Steepest Dowries
content: Education, ironically, is more of an enabler of the practice than a hindrance, per a 2023 study quoted by the BBC

title: Live Updates: "Son Of Retired Judge Being Protected": Twisha Sharma's Lawyer
content: Twisha Sharma Death Updates: The case came under scrutiny as the Madhya Pradesh Police faced criticism for an inefficient probe into the case, while Giribala Singh was accused of exerting undue influence.

title: Karnataka Government Withdraws Riot Cases, BJP Says 'Violence Encouraged'
content: Reacting sharply to the move, BJP spokesperson S. Prakash accused the Congress government of encouraging violent elements.

title: Adani Group's Proposed US Investment To Boost India-US Economic Ties: Experts
content: Meanwhile, Adapa Prasad, president of BJP USA, said the withdrawal of the case by US prosecutors was significant because authorities acknowledged that they did not have sufficient evidence.

title: WHO Africa Head Says Underestimating Ebola Spread Risk Can Be "Big Mistake"
content: WHO regional director for Africa Mohamed Yakub Janabi said just one case of Ebola could spread the virus beyond the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.

title: Parents Who Bought NEET Papers Under CBI Scanner, Doctor Questioned
content: Manoj Shirure, a senior paediatrician in Latur, allegedly bought the leaked question papers for his son.

title: Opinion: Opinion | On Memes, Wars, And Meme-Wars
content: The meme 'warfare' feels different from traditional forms of propaganda. It is participatory. People are not just consuming the messaging; they are helping spread it themselves.

title: Gujarat's Frozen Food Train To Supply KFC, Burger King Across Asia
content: The first train was flagged off on Thursday for HyFun Foods, one of India’s major frozen food exporters.

title: 'If Parents Are IAS Officers, Why Reservation For Their Children?': Supreme Court
content: Before the case reached the Supreme Court, the authorities noted that both parents were government employees and that their combined income exceeded the prescribed creamy layer threshold

title: Bhagwant Mann Faces Backlash Over 'Misinterpreted' Top Court Dog Ruling
content: Punjab Governor Gulab Chand Kataria also urged caution, stating that any such exercise must strictly follow Supreme Court guidelines.

title: Opinion: Opinion | India's Oil Reserves: How Long Can They Last As Iran War Continues?
content: From a future energy security perspective, India will have to significantly enhance its SPR capacities from 17 days to a minimum of 45 days on land, with the possibility of another 10-15 days of SPR on sea on tankers.

title: 'Dark Nightmare': Commuters After 50-Minute Rush-Hour Glitch On Mumbai Metro
content: Passengers reported experiencing immense mental stress and inconvenience during this period and were unable to even establish contact with Metro staff

title: Video: Giribala Singh's Lawyer Bangs On Car, Shouts As Reporters Surround Him
content: Enosh George's anger increased manifold as his foot came under the tyre of the car in which Giribala Singh was sitting.

title: 'Say Jai Shri Ram In Attendance Calls': Karnataka BJP Leaders Amid Hijab Debate
content: The hijab controversy first erupted in Karnataka in 2022 after Muslim students in Udupi protested restrictions on wearing hijabs inside classrooms

title: NPS Swasthya Explained: Can It Replace Your Health Insurance?
content: Experts say NPS Swasthya is not designed to replace health insurance. Instead, it may work best as a second layer of financial protection.

title: Rent hike rules: Can landlords increase rent without notice in India?
